subject: biology
educational_personality: exceptional classroom teacher; cream textbook; diagram teaches

lesson.big_idea: Digestion breaks food into smaller pieces the body can absorb. Each organ has a clear job.

sections.title: Have You Wondered?
sections.body: How does a bite of bread become energy your body can use? Digestion is the journey.

sections.title: Using the Diagram
sections.body: Follow Mouth → Stomach → Small intestine. Each stop changes food so nutrients can enter the blood.

sections.title: Concept: Digestion
sections.body: Digestion is the breakdown of food. Mechanical chewing and chemical juices work together.

sections.title: Say It Simply
sections.body: Your body takes big food pieces and turns them into tiny nutrients it can absorb.

sections.title: In Real Life
sections.body: Chewing a chapati thoroughly helps digestion start in the mouth before the stomach continues the work.

sections.title: Worked Example
sections.body: Track a rice grain: teeth crush it, saliva moistens it, the stomach mixes it, and the small intestine absorbs nutrients.

sections.title: Try This
sections.body: Point to Small intestine and explain why absorption matters.

sections.title: Lesson Summary
sections.body: Digestion is a team effort. Food is broken down step by step so nutrients can feed your cells.

sections.title: Think About It
sections.body: Why do doctors ask you to chew slowly? Connect that advice to today's lesson.

sections.title: Apply Your Learning
sections.body: Make a labelled journey map of one meal through the digestive system.

diagram_package.title: Digestive System
diagram_package.caption: Mouth → Stomach → Intestine
diagram_package.explanation: This diagram teaches how the ideas in Digestive System connect.
diagram_package.practice_question: Point to Mouth and explain two ways digestion begins there.
